questions.stem: Water flows from a pipe at 211.0 kPa, 2 m/s at elevation 10m to ground level. Neglecting losses, the pressure (kPa) at ground level (velocity 24.9 m/s) is:

options: NOx formation by lowering peak temperature
questions.solution: EGR: dilutes charge with exhaust. Lower peak T reduces NOx.

questions.solution: Annealing heats and slowly cools steel, softening it and relieving stresses.

questions.subject: Mechanical Engineering
questions.stem: Nusselt number represents:
options: Ratio of convective to conductive heat transfer
questions.solution: Nu = hL/k. Higher Nu = more convection relative to conduction.

questions.stem: Moody chart:
options: f vs Re and ε/D (relative roughness). Laminar: f = 64/Re. Turbulent: Colebrook equation

questions.stem: If the diameter of a solid circular shaft is doubled (same material, same shear stress), the torque it can transmit increases by a factor of about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mild steel column of diameter 25mm and length 3.0m is pinned at both ends. E = 200000.0 MPa. The Euler buckling load (kN) is approximately:
questions.solution: P_cr = π²EI/L²; I = πd⁴/64 = 19175 mm⁴; P_cr = 4.21 kN

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Yield strength vs ultimate strength:
options: Yield: onset of plastic deformation. Ultimate: maximum stress before necking
questions.solution: For steel: σ_y ≈ 250 MPa, σ_u ≈ 400 MPa. For brittle: σ_u at fracture.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Maximum distortion energy theory (von Mises):
questions.solution: von Mises: more accurate for ductile materials. σ_vM = √(½[(σ₁-σ₂)²+(σ₂-σ₃)²+(σ₃-σ₁)²]).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Enthalpy h is defined as:
questions.solution: Enthalpy h = u + pv, a convenient property for flow processes.

questions.subject: Mechanical Engineering
questions.stem: Governor sensitivity:
options: Ratio of change in equilibrium speed to change in sleeve lift
questions.solution: Sensitivity = dN/dh. More sensitive = smaller speed change.
